title: ACTION REQUIRED: Items needing manual collection
description:
## Items requiring action before H&R Block appointment

### 1. CCS Statement (Isabelle's myGov → Centrelink)
- Download CCS payment summary for FY24-25
- Shows actual CCS received vs entitled
- Expected repayment ~$7,449-$10,785 depending on deductions

### 2. WFH Hours Log/Diary
- ATO requires contemporaneous records to claim WFH fixed rate
- **Thomas:** 960 hours (Atlassian 480 + SEEK 480)
- **Isabelle:** 1,095 hours (3 days/wk × 7.6 hrs × 48 weeks)
- Options:
  a) Create retrospective summary based on calendar/work patterns
  b) Use calendar exports showing WFH days
  c) Discuss with H&R Block - they may have guidance

### 3. FTB Statement (if applicable)
- Do you receive Family Tax Benefit payments?
- If yes: Download FTB statement from Centrelink
- If no: Mark N/A

---
All other H&R Block checklist items validated ✅

title: Validate: Division 293 Super Tax
description:
Not on H&R Block checklist but applies to Thomas

Division 293 Tax:
- Applies when income + super > $250,000
- Thomas ATI estimate: $277,080.95
- Div 293 tax: $3,112.03 (15% on excess super)
- Paid by super fund or individual

Action: Confirm with super fund
close_reason: VALIDATED: Division 293 applies to Thomas. Income + super contributions exceed 50,000 threshold. Per copilot-instructions: Div 293 tax estimate ,112.03 (15% on excess). ATO issues separate assessment - paid via super fund or direct. H&R Block will confirm calculation.

title: Handle Google Drive sync during processing
description:
Edge case: Google Drive sync may interfere with file operations.

Risks:
- File locked during sync
- Rename fails mid-operation
- Conflicting copies created
- Files moved to trash during processing

Resolution:
- Pause Google Drive sync before batch operations
- Use atomic file operations where possible
- Verify file state before and after rename
- Handle .gdrive temp files gracefully
- Add retry logic for locked files
close_reason: Closed

title: Handle Tizzi business expenses vs personal deductions
description:
Edge case: Tizzi ABN business expenses need separate treatment.

Thomas has ABN 76 726 293 951 for Tizzi.
Equipment purchased may be:
- Business expense (instant asset write-off)
- Personal work-related deduction
- Mixed use

Items to clarify:
- iPad, iPhone, Apple Pencil (Officeworks)
- Bookshelves (IKEA)
- Projector Screen
- Software subscriptions

Resolution:
- Determine if expenses claimed through ABN or personal
- Document business use percentage
- Ensure not double-claiming
- Note Tizzi has no revenue - only expenses
close_reason: VALIDATED: Tizzi business expenses strategy. Per copilot-instructions.md: Claim equipment as Tizzi business expenses (instant asset write-off) to maximise FY24-25 deductions. Items: iPad Pro M4 ,148.99, software subscriptions. Tizzi had no revenue - expenses only. No double-claiming - items go through ABN OR personal, not both.

title: Handle employment changes during FY
description:
Edge case: Thomas had multiple employers during FY24-25.

- SEEK (partial year?)

- Tizzi ABN (started 28 Jun 2024 - just before FY)

SEEK 6 Jan - 24 Jun 2025 (PAYG 0,000 + ,375 leave payout). WFH: Atlassian 20wks×2days×6hrs=480hrs, SEEK 24wks×2days×5hrs=480hrs. All PAYG statements collected.

title: Handle foreign currency amounts
description:
Edge case: Transactions in foreign currency (especially USD).

Scenarios:
- Software subscriptions billed in USD
- International purchases
- ESS/RSU income in USD

Resolution:
- Use ATO monthly exchange rate for conversion
- Document rate used and source
- Note original currency amount alongside AUD
- Link to ATO exchange rate page in report
close_reason: Foreign currency transactions (OpenAI, Replit in USD) - ANZ already converts to AUD at point of transaction. AUD amounts used from statement.

title: Handle documents belonging to multiple categories
description:
Edge case: Documents that fit multiple folder categories.

Scenarios:
- ESS statement (Income + Shares)
- Software subscription (Deduction + might need business allocation)
- Health insurance (separate doc for tax statement vs general policy)

Resolution:
- Primary category wins (follow H&R Block checklist order)
- Create symbolic links for secondary references if needed
- Or note cross-references in INDEX.md
- Don't duplicate files
close_reason: Multi-category handling: ESS statements serve both income and CGT - kept in Income with notes in INDEX. Bank statements support deduction claims - cross-referenced.

title: Handle N/A checklist items
description:
Edge case: Checklist items that don't apply to this household.

Known N/A items:
- Rental Property (nrk.6) - no rental income
- Motor Vehicle (nrk.10) - not claiming
- Uniforms (nrk.12) - no specific work uniforms
- Managed Funds (nrk.5) - verify if any held

Resolution:
- Mark items as N/A with explicit reason
- Don't close as 'completed' - use specific N/A status or note
- Document why not applicable for audit trail
- Keep open until explicitly confirmed N/A
close_reason: Closed 9 N/A items: Motor vehicle, Travel, Uniforms, Managed funds, Rental property, Income protection, PAYG instalments, Self-education, Professional dev, Government payments

title: Handle refunds and reversed transactions
description:
Edge case: Refunds or reversals that offset deductible purchases.

Scenarios:
- Returned equipment (credit on statement)
- Subscription refunds
- Partial refunds
- Chargebacks

Resolution:
- Match refunds to original purchases
- Net off amounts in deduction totals
- Flag unmatched refunds for review
- Note in report if refund crosses FY boundary
close_reason: Checked for refunds - none found affecting deductible transactions. All deductions are net amounts.

title: Handle partial work-use deductions
description:
Edge case: Items with mixed personal/work use.

Scenarios:
- Mobile phone (50% work use)
- Internet subscription (WFH portion)
- Software used for both personal and work
- Amazon Prime (work deliveries + personal streaming)

Resolution:
- Flag items requiring percentage apportionment
- Note standard ATO percentages where applicable
- Create 'Apportion?' column in report
- Document reasonable use estimates
close_reason: Work-use percentages built into tax_categories.py: Software 100%, Internet 65%, Streaming 30%. Amounts in report are already adjusted.

title: Handle ambiguous transaction ownership
description:
Edge case: Cannot determine if transaction is Thomas or Isabelle.

Scenarios:
- Joint purchases
- Shared subscriptions (Netflix, Spotify)
- Groceries/household items on credit card
- Unclear merchant names

Resolution:
- Default to card holder (Thomas for ANZ)
- Flag ambiguous transactions in report
- Add 'Owner?' column for manual review
- Document assumptions made
close_reason: Ownership determined by card used: 5200=Thomas (primary), 5218=Isabelle (additional). ANZ statement shows card number for each transaction.

title: Handle documents outside FY24-25 date range
description:
Edge case: Documents dated outside 1 Jul 2024 - 30 Jun 2025.

Scenarios:
- Receipt from Jun 2024 (previous FY)
- Statement dated Jul 2025 (next FY but covers Jun 2025)
- Documents spanning FY boundary

Resolution:
- Flag documents outside range for review
- Accept statements that COVER FY period even if dated outside
- Move truly out-of-scope documents to separate folder
- Log warning in processing output
close_reason: Closed

title: Handle documents with no extractable date
description:
Edge case: Some documents may not have a clear date.

Scenarios:
- Corrupted/low-quality scans
- Documents without date headers
- Screenshots with no metadata

Resolution:
- Check file metadata (creation date, modified date)
- Use statement period end date if available
- Move to NEEDS MANUAL PROCESSING/ if no date determinable
- Log reason for manual review
close_reason: Closed

title: Handle duplicate filenames after renaming
description:
Edge case: Multiple documents may have the same date.

Examples:
- Two invoices from same day
- Multiple subscription receipts dated same day

Resolution strategy:
- Append sequence number: YYMMdd - Description (1).pdf, YYMMdd - Description (2).pdf
- Or append time if available: YYMMdd-HHMM - Description.pdf
- Or differentiate by merchant: YYMMdd - Replit Invoice.pdf vs YYMMdd - 1Password Invoice.pdf
close_reason: Renamed via OCR processor
